narration: Linear search checks each item one by one until it finds the target.
callout: This works even when the array is not sorted.

narration: If the first values are not the target, the search simply keeps moving to the next spot.
decision: At index 2, the target 7 is found.

predictionPrompt.prompt: What should linear search return if the target never appears?

choices.feedback: Correct. That shows the scan finished without finding the target.
Not this one. Index 0 would mean the target was found at the start.

narration: Linear search is simple and dependable when the data is small or unsorted.
callout: Sometimes the simplest search is the right one.
